cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user is asking why foreign kids want to learn Chinese. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go back to the provided text and find the relevant information. The passage starts by mentioning that while English is getting more important in schools, Chinese is popular among foreign kids. Then it gives an example of Piao Chenglong from South Korea who wants to learn Chinese to study at Peking University. It also states that in South Korea, over 300,000 people are learning Chinese, and some start as early as their first day at school. The text then expands to mention that Chinese is popular worldwide, with 130 million people in 85 countries learning it, and the number is expected to reach 800 million. In America, Chinese is the second most popular foreign language after Spanish. The reasons given for wanting to learn Chinese are that China is becoming an important country, foreign countries want to understand China better for business, and the Chinese government is sending teachers and setting up Confucius Colleagues to teach Chinese. Looking at the options: A) Because Chinese is getting more important in their schools. The passage actually says English is getting more important in schools, but Chinese is popular among foreign kids. So A might not be correct. B) Because Chinese characters have too many strokes. The text mentions that Piao finds Chinese characters have too many strokes and English is easier for him. But he still wants to learn Chinese because he wants to study at Peking University. So B seems to be a negative point, not the reason why they want to learn it. C) Because the number of people learning Chinese will be 800 million.
